behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA is explicit: this behavior depends on real filesystem watcher semantics for nested paths (OS event emission + recursive watch behavior). A fake watcher can hide failures where subdirectory events are never emitted or not subscribed. Rejection-gate: Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because behavior text contains no AI/ML decision and no multi-agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Start the daemon against a real temporary directory tree, create a nested subdirectory, write a file there, and assert exactly one transformed output is produced from that nested file path.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS points to local logic: behavior text is specifically about dedup decision by path+time window, not OS/network/subprocess. A deterministic clock and in-memory state can reproduce the failure. Rejection-gate: Integration was considered but disqualified because the behavior does not explicitly require cross-module handoff; it is the dedup rule itself.
behaviors.test_description: Call the dedup component twice with the same normalized file path within the configured window using a controlled clock; assert first call is accepted and second is rejected, then accepted again after the window expires.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text explicitly names OS-emitted duplicate events; REAL-INFRA boundary is the failure source. In-process event stubs can miss real burst patterns/coalescing differences, so MOCK-HIDE applies. Rejection-gate: Agentic/Workflow disqualified because no model output dependency or multi-agent sequence is described.
behaviors.test_description: Run the daemon with a real watched directory, perform a write pattern known to trigger multiple events on the target OS (e.g., write+rename/update), and assert only one processing/output record for that file.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: This is an OS filesystem lifecycle condition (delete/recreate inode/path while watcher is active). REAL-INFRA is required because watcher invalidation/rebind behavior is platform/runtime dependent; mocks often hide it. Rejection-gate: higher levels (Agentic/Workflow) are disqualified by absence of AI/ML decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon on a real directory, delete the watched directory, recreate it at the same path, then add a new file and assert it is processed without restarting the daemon.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is ordering under concurrent arrival driven by real filesystem timestamps/events. REAL-INFRA likely matters because creation-time resolution/event ordering can differ on actual filesystems and hide bugs with fakes. Rejection-gate: Agentic/Workflow disqualified because behavior is deterministic daemon ordering, not model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Create several files nearly simultaneously in a real watched directory with controlled/known creation times, then assert processing order matches the extracted creation timestamp order.

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on timing between real write-in-progress state and filesystem arrival events. REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE both apply: in-process fakes usually cannot reproduce partial-write race conditions accurately. Rejection-gate: Agentic/Workflow disqualified because no AI/ML output dependency is present.
behaviors.test_description: Begin a long/streamed write to a file in the watched directory, ensure arrival event occurs before write completion, and assert processing starts only after writer closes/flushing completes and output reflects full file content.
